title: Legacy001 ablate count encoding
summary: Remove count encoding from the legacy-001 feature pipeline as an isolated ablation.
rationale: The root code includes count encodings for categorical values. These may provide useful frequency priors, but they can also duplicate category information or over-emphasize dataset composition. A clean ablation measures whether count encoding is a net positive.
implementation_hint: Start from legacy-001.py. Disable only the count-encoding block and remove the resulting count columns from the feature set. Keep floor categories, manual interactions, bins, combo target encoding, RealMLP settings, folds, and artifacts unchanged.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if count encoding is adding noisy or unstable frequency information.
risk: Count encoding may be one of the useful compact categorical signals in the root baseline.
